messages.content:
1. Brightness filter: This filter can be used to adjust the brightness of the text while maintaining the contrast between the text and background. A value of 80-90% would work well for night mode.

2. Contrast filter: This filter can be used to increase the contrast between the text and background. A value of 150-200% would make the text more legible.

3. Saturation filter: This filter can be used to reduce the saturation of the text color. A value of around 30-40% would desaturate the text without making it look washed out.

4. Blur filter: This filter can be used to soften the edges of the text and make it less harsh on the eyes. A value of around 3-4px would work well for this.

5. Grayscale filter: This filter can be used to make the text color grayscale. This can make the text easier to read in night mode. A value of around 90-95% would work well for this.
how about some color mix modes filters?

Color mix mode filters could be a great addition to the text-compare night background mode effects. Here are some possible filters to consider:

1. Overlay: This filter can be applied to the text to blend its color with the background. The text color becomes more transparent and the background color shows through. This filter can help to create a cohesive design and improve the readability of the text. A value of 50-70% would work well for this filter.

2. Color Dodge: This filter can be used to lighten the text and make it more visible against a dark background. The text color becomes more vibrant and stands out from the background. A value of around 20-40% would work well for this filter.

3. Color Burn: This filter can be used to darken the text and make it more visible against a light background. The text color becomes more intense and makes the text more legible. A value of around 20-40% would work well for this filter.

4. Difference: This filter can be used to invert the color of the text and make it stand out from the background. The text becomes more contrasting and easier to read. A value of around 60-80% would work well for this filter.

5. Screen: This filter can be used to lighten the text and make it more visible against a dark background. The text color becomes more transparent and blends with the background. A value of around 20-40% would work well for this filter.
"The text color becomes more transparent and improve the readability of the text." this not correlate with each another.

You are right, that statement had a contradiction. Let me clarify:

"The text color becomes more transparent and the text blends with the background." This filter can be applied to the text in order to create a cohesive design and improve the overall readability of the text.

I apologize for the confusion. Thank you for bringing it to my attention.

what is standard?

The `scrollbar-width` property does not have a standard or default value. However, most browsers use a default scrollbar size that is approximately equivalent to the width of `17px` - `18px`. 

If you set `scrollbar-width` to a value of `2em`, this would translate to a width of `32px` for 16px text (which is the default font size in most browsers). 

Note that choosing a very wide scrollbar may interfere with other elements on the page and make it less user-friendly. It's important to balance aesthetic design with usability and ensure that the scrollbar remains functional and easy to use for all users.
